chief_complaint: A previously healthy 6-month-old male infant presented with a 23-day history of fever and seizures.
history: The infant received the mumps vaccine (Hoshino strain) 23 days prior to the onset of symptoms. He had a history of normal development and no prior medical conditions. The infant was fully vaccinated and had no known allergies.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, heart rate 160/min, respiratory rate 40/min. Physical examination revealed a lethargic infant with aseptic meningitis signs.
labs: Cerebrospinal fluid analysis: lymphocytic pleocytosis, normal glucose, and elevated protein. Reverse transcription-PCR and sequencing confirmed the presence of the vaccine strain.
correct_diagnosis: Febrile status epilepticus with aseptic meningitis due to mumps vaccine
differentials: Febrile status epilepticus with aseptic meningitis due to mumps vaccine
correct_ranking: Febrile status epilepticus with aseptic meningitis due to mumps vaccine
